hypothesis: If a chemically informed autoencoder latent space and conditional latent diffusion model (CompGen) are used—with conditioning on adsorbate identity, adsorption energy, and allowed elements and with pretraining on OC20 followed by HEA fine-tuning—then the framework will generate shell-wise catalytic surface compositions that achieve lower Fréchet Distance to target distributions, lower Leakage (better constraint compliance), and higher Cosine Similarity to ground-truth compositions than chemically-agnostic baselines while requiring less HEA training data.
